title: Selaginella polystachya
taxonomy: Selaginella polystachya belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that are thought to be among the most ancient living things on Earth. The species is further classified as a member of the family Selaginellaceae, a family of lycopods that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. As for its order, Selaginella polystachya is classified under Selaginellales. Within the realm of kingdom, it falls under Plantae, which is the broad category that encompasses all types of flowering and non-flowering plants. At the most basic level of classification, Selaginella polystachya is a species within the genus Selaginella.
uses: Selaginella polystachya has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have wound-healing properties and has been used to treat various skin conditions. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antioxidant and antifungal activities. Traditional practitioners have used Selaginella polystachya to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.

intro: Selaginella polystachya, also known as Polyskys spikemoss, is a type of resurrection plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae, characterized by its ability to survive in water-deprived conditions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent infections. Bioactive constituents of Selaginella polystachya include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
